what: The three datasets at a glance
title: Getting started with olympicAthletes
text: olympic_athletes headline long-format table. medal_table editions companion tables make per-team-event medal counting edition-level lookups easy without re-aggregating 300k+ rows.

title: Getting started with olympicAthletes
text: quick sanity check — participations per Games, season:
code: data(olympic_athletes) str(olympic_athletes, give.attr = FALSE, vec.len = 2) #> 'data.frame':    315090 obs. of  16 variables: #>  $ id              : int  12068 35094 35698 38123 41160 ... #>  $ name            : chr  "Arthur Charles Blake" "Angelos Fetsis" ... #>  $ sex             : chr  "M" "M" ... #>  $ age             : int  24 NA 22 23 21 ... #>  $ height          : num  NA NA NA 154 NA ... #>  $ weight          : num  NA NA NA 45 NA ... #>  $ team            : chr  "United States" "Greece" ... #>  $ noc             : chr  "USA" "GRE" ... #>  $ games           : chr  "1896 Summer" "1896 Summer" ... #>  $ year            : int  1896 1896 1896 1896 1896 ... #>  $ season          : chr  "Summer" "Summer" ... #>  $ city_local_latin: chr  "Athína" "Athína" ... #>  $ city_english    : chr  "Athens" "Athens" ... #>  $ sport           : chr  "Athletics" "Athletics" ... #>  $ event           : chr  "Athletics Men's 1,500 metres" "Athletics Men's 1,500 metres" ... #>  $ medal           : chr  "Silver" NA ... tab <- table(olympic_athletes$year, olympic_athletes$season) tail(tab, 10) #>        #>        Summer Winter #>   2008  13602      0 #>   2010      0   4402 #>   2012  12920      0 #>   2014      0   4891 #>   2016  13688      0 #>   2018      0   5119 #>   2020  14536      0 #>   2022      0   5247 #>   2024  13659      0 #>   2026      0   5413

title: Getting started with olympicAthletes
text: Medals olympic_athletes per-player: every member gold-winning ice hockey roster medal = "Gold" row. right shape athlete-level analysis, inflates team counts sum naively. headline medal-table totals, use medal_table instead.
code: # Every roster member of the men's ice hockey gold medal team # at Beijing 2022 gets a Gold row: hockey_2022 <- subset(   olympic_athletes,   year == 2022 &     event == "Ice Hockey Men's Ice Hockey" &     medal == "Gold" ) nrow(hockey_2022) #> [1] 0

title: Getting started with olympicAthletes
text: medal_table covers every Olympic edition Athens 1896 Milano-Cortina 2026 uses IOC per-team-event convention. Top five NOCs Paris 2024 gold count:
code: data(medal_table) head(subset(medal_table, year == 2024), 5) #>      edition_id       games year season noc                    country gold #> 1808         63 2024 Summer 2024 Summer USA              United States   40 #> 1809         63 2024 Summer 2024 Summer CHN People's Republic of China   40 #> 1810         63 2024 Summer 2024 Summer JPN                      Japan   20 #> 1811         63 2024 Summer 2024 Summer AUS                  Australia   18 #> 1812         63 2024 Summer 2024 Summer FRA                     France   16 #>      silver bronze total notes #> 1808     44     42   126       #> 1809     27     24    91       #> 1810     12     13    45       #> 1811     19     16    53       #> 1812     26     22    64 paris <- subset(medal_table, year == 2024) paris <- paris[order(-paris$gold, -paris$total), ] head(paris[, c("noc", "country", "gold", "silver", "bronze", "total")], 5) #>      noc                    country gold silver bronze total #> 1808 USA              United States   40     44     42   126 #> 1809 CHN People's Republic of China   40     27     24    91 #> 1810 JPN                      Japan   20     12     13    45 #> 1811 AUS                  Australia   18     19     16    53 #> 1812 FRA                     France   16     26     22    64

title: Getting started with olympicAthletes
text: editions includes one row per Olympic edition (62 total), including handful Games cancelled World Wars. Use enrich olympic_athletes edition-level facts (host country, opening date, total medal events) without scraping anything .
code: data(editions) head(editions[, c("games", "city_local_latin", "city_english", "country",                   "opening_ceremony", "closing_ceremony",                   "participants", "medal_events")], 10) #>          games city_local_latin city_english       country opening_ceremony #> 1  1896 Summer           Athína       Athens        Greece       1896-04-06 #> 2  1900 Summer            Paris        Paris        France             <NA> #> 3  1904 Summer        St. Louis    St. Louis United States       1904-05-14 #> 4  1906 Summer           Athína       Athens        Greece       1906-04-22 #> 5  1908 Summer           London       London Great Britain       1908-07-13 #> 6  1912 Summer        Stockholm    Stockholm        Sweden       1912-07-06 #> 7  1916 Summer           Berlin       Berlin       Germany             <NA> #> 8  1920 Summer        Antwerpen      Antwerp       Belgium       1920-08-14 #> 9  1924 Summer            Paris        Paris        France       1924-07-05 #> 10 1924 Winter         Chamonix     Chamonix        France       1924-01-24 #>    closing_ceremony participants medal_events #> 1        1896-04-15          176           43 #> 2              <NA>         1241           95 #> 3              <NA>          650           95 #> 4        1906-05-02          841           74 #> 5        1908-07-25         2024          110 #> 6        1912-07-15         2409          107 #> 7              <NA>           NA           NA #> 8        1920-08-30         2680          162 #> 9        1924-07-27         3255          131 #> 10       1924-02-05          312           17

title: Getting started with olympicAthletes
text: 1896-2016 rows come rgriff23/Olympic_history, originally scraped sports-reference.com. 2018-2026 rows scraped olympedia.org using pipeline ismayc/olympic-moderndive-data. build script data-raw/DATASET.R reproduces every .rda data/ CSVs data-raw/. See ?olympic_athletes, ?medal_table, ?editions full column-level documentation.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Chester Ismay. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Ismay C (2026). olympicAthletes: Olympic Athlete Event Data, Athens 1896 Milano-Cortina 2026. R package version 0.3.0, https://github.com/moderndive/olympicAthletes.
code: @Manual{,   title = {olympicAthletes: Olympic Athlete Event Data, Athens 1896 to Milano-Cortina 2026},   author = {Chester Ismay},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.3.0},   url = {https://github.com/moderndive/olympicAthletes}, }

title: olympicAthletes • Athens 1896 to Milano-Cortina 2026
text: tidy R data package covering every athlete-event participation modern Olympic Games, Athens 1896 Milano-Cortina 2026 — 315,000 rows.

title: olympicAthletes • Athens 1896 to Milano-Cortina 2026
text: olympic_athletes extends original rgriff23/Olympic_history dataset. Column names lowercase snake_case (e.g. noc, medal) rather upstream TitleCase, host-city column split city_local_latin city_english. city_local_latin always uses Latin alphabet — non-Latin-script cities romanized ("Moskva", "Tokyo", "Beijing", "PyeongChang"), rendered native script.
code: library(olympicAthletes)  data(olympic_athletes)   # ~315,000 athlete-event rows, 1896-2026 data(medal_table)        # verified medal table for every edition, 1896-2026 data(editions)           # metadata for every Games, 1896-2026  ?olympic_athletes

title: olympicAthletes • Athens 1896 to Milano-Cortina 2026
text: Rows 1896-2016 come rgriff23 dataset (originally scraped sports-reference.com). Rows 2018-2026 scraped olympedia.org using pipeline olympic-moderndive-data repository. See data-raw/DATASET.R reproducible build.

title: olympicAthletes • Athens 1896 to Milano-Cortina 2026
text: Medal counts per-player, per-team-event. team gold (e.g. Ice Hockey Men) produces one medal = "Gold" row per roster member. Group medal noc divide team-event medals team size reproduce IOC medal table — use medal_table companion dataset uses IOC convention. Bio coverage (height/weight) ranges ~80% older Winter Games ~25% Paris 2024 / Milano-Cortina 2026. Olympedia bios newer less-prominent athletes sparse — pattern original 1896-2016 source.

title: olympicAthletes • Athens 1896 to Milano-Cortina 2026
text: CC 4.0. Please cite package, rgriff23 source, Olympedia.

title: olympicAthletes • Athens 1896 to Milano-Cortina 2026
code: Ismay, C. (2026). olympicAthletes: Olympic Athlete Event Data, Athens 1896 to Milano-Cortina 2026. R package version 0.3.0.  Griffin, R. (2018). Olympic history: longitudinal data scraped from www.sports-reference.com. https://github.com/rgriff23/Olympic_history  OlyMADMen. Olympedia. https://www.olympedia.org/

what: Edition-level metadata for every Olympic Games, 1896-2026 — editions
title: Edition-level metadata for every Olympic Games, 1896-2026 — editions
text: One row per Olympic edition (including cancelled Games) verified totals participants, NOCs, medal events, disciplines, plus opening/closing ceremony dates.

title: Edition-level metadata for every Olympic Games, 1896-2026 — editions
Edition-level metadata for every Olympic Games, 1896-2026 — editions
text: data frame 62 rows 15 variables: edition_id Integer. Olympedia's internal edition number. games Character. e.g. "2024 Summer". year Integer. season Character. "Summer" "Winter". city_local_latin Character. Host city local-language     form rendered Latin alphabet — .e. native Latin-script     names retain diacritics ("Athína", "München",     "Montréal", "Ciudad de México"), non-Latin-script     names use standard romanization rather city's native     script ("Moskva" "Москва", "Tokyo"     "東京", "Beijing" "北京",     "PyeongChang" "평창"). column therefore     always ASCII--Latin-1 never contains city's     native non-Latin alphabet. city_english Character. Common English name host city     (e.g. "Athens", "Munich", "Moscow",     "Mexico City"). country Character. opening_ceremony, closing_ceremony Date NA. ISO-8601.     NA cancelled editions early Games     Olympedia record exact dates. participants Integer NA. Olympedia's headline     participant count. NA cancelled editions. nocs Integer NA. Number NOCs represented. medal_events Integer NA. Number medal events. disciplines Integer NA. Number distinct disciplines. notes Character. Free-text edition notes (e.g. cancellation     reasons, intercalated-Games status). source Character. Olympedia URL.

title: Edition-level metadata for every Olympic Games, 1896-2026 — editions
text: https://www.olympedia.org/

what: Verified medal tables for every Olympic edition, 1896-2026 — medal_table
title: Verified medal tables for every Olympic edition, 1896-2026 — medal_table
text: One row per (Games, NOC) verified Gold/Silver/Bronze counts cross- checked olympedia.org Wikipedia/IOC. Use rather aggregating olympic_athletes want per-team-event medal convention (one medal per gold-winning ice hockey team, 25).

title: Verified medal tables for every Olympic edition, 1896-2026 — medal_table
Verified medal tables for every Olympic edition, 1896-2026 — medal_table
text: data frame 1,929 rows 11 variables: edition_id Integer. Olympedia's internal edition number. games Character. e.g. "2026 Winter". year Integer. season Character. "Summer" "Winter". noc Character. Three-letter IOC code. country Character. Full country/team name. gold, silver, bronze Integer. Medal counts. total Integer. Sum three. notes Character. Free-text notes (e.g. shared medals,     post-hoc reallocations).

title: Verified medal tables for every Olympic edition, 1896-2026 — medal_table
text: https://www.olympedia.org/ (manually verified per edition).

title: Verified medal tables for every Olympic edition, 1896-2026 — medal_table
code: data(medal_table) subset(medal_table, year == 2024)[1:10, ] #>      edition_id       games year season noc                    country gold #> 1808         63 2024 Summer 2024 Summer USA              United States   40 #> 1809         63 2024 Summer 2024 Summer CHN People's Republic of China   40 #> 1810         63 2024 Summer 2024 Summer JPN                      Japan   20 #> 1811         63 2024 Summer 2024 Summer AUS                  Australia   18 #> 1812         63 2024 Summer 2024 Summer FRA                     France   16 #> 1813         63 2024 Summer 2024 Summer NED                Netherlands   15 #> 1814         63 2024 Summer 2024 Summer GBR              Great Britain   14 #> 1815         63 2024 Summer 2024 Summer KOR          Republic of Korea   13 #> 1816         63 2024 Summer 2024 Summer ITA                      Italy   12 #> 1817         63 2024 Summer 2024 Summer GER                    Germany   12 #>      silver bronze total notes #> 1808     44     42   126       #> 1809     27     24    91       #> 1810     12     13    45       #> 1811     19     16    53       #> 1812     26     22    64       #> 1813      7     12    34       #> 1814     22     29    65       #> 1815      9     10    32       #> 1816     13     15    40       #> 1817     13      8    33

what: olympicAthletes: Olympic Athlete Event Data, Athens 1896 to Milano-Cortina 2026 — olympicAthletes-package
title: olympicAthletes: Olympic Athlete Event Data, Athens 1896 to Milano-Cortina 2026 — olympicAthletes-package
text: tidy, long-format dataset every athlete-event participation modern Olympic Games. Extends rgriff23 Olympic_history dataset (1896-2016) five additional editions scraped olympedia.org: PyeongChang 2018, Tokyo 2020, Beijing 2022, Paris 2024, Milano-Cortina 2026.

title: olympicAthletes: Olympic Athlete Event Data, Athens 1896 to Milano-Cortina 2026 — olympicAthletes-package
text: olympic_athletes: ~315,000 athlete-event rows. medal_table: verified medal tables every     edition, 1896-2026. editions: edition-level metadata every     Games, 1896-2026.

title: olympicAthletes: Olympic Athlete Event Data, Athens 1896 to Milano-Cortina 2026 — olympicAthletes-package
text: Maintainer: Chester Ismay chester.ismay@gmail.com

title: Olympic athlete-event participations, 1896-2026 — olympic_athletes
text: One row per (athlete, Games, event) participation across every modern Summer Winter Olympic Games Athens 1896 Milano-Cortina 2026. Rows 1896-2016 come rgriff23 Olympic_history dataset (originally scraped sports-reference.com); rows 2018-2026 scraped olympedia.org.

title: Olympic athlete-event participations, 1896-2026 — olympic_athletes
text: data frame 315,090 rows 16 variables: id Integer. Unique athlete ID. Original IDs 1..135571;     athletes new 2018-2026 editions get IDs starting     1,000,000 avoid collision. name Character. ASCII-folded athlete name. sex Character. "M" "F". age Integer NA. Approximate age start     Games, computed date birth (Feb 1 reference Winter,     Jul 15 Summer). height Numeric NA. Height centimetres. weight Numeric NA. Weight kilograms. team Character. Country/team name displayed source. noc Character. Three-letter IOC code (e.g. "USA",     "", "ROC"). games Character. "<year> <Summer/Winter>", e.g.     "2026 Winter". year Integer. Calendar year Games. Tokyo 2020     coded 2020 even though held 2021 (IOC convention). season Character. "Summer" "Winter". city_local_latin Character. Host city local-language     form rendered Latin alphabet — .e. native Latin-script     names retain diacritics ("Athína", "München",     "Montréal", "Ciudad de México"), non-Latin-script     names use standard romanization rather city's native     script ("Moskva" "Москва", "Tokyo"     "東京", "Beijing" "北京",     "PyeongChang" "평창"). column therefore     always ASCII--Latin-1 never contains city's     native non-Latin alphabet. city_english Character. Common English name host city     (e.g. "Athens", "Munich", "Moscow",     "Mexico City"). sport Character. Sport name. 2018-2026 rows normalised     original sport names possible (e.g. "Cycling Track"     "Cycling Road" collapse "Cycling"). event Character. Event name including sport prefix. medal Character NA. "Gold", "Silver",     "Bronze", NA. Per-player: every member     medal-winning team roster gets row team's medal.

title: Olympic athlete-event participations, 1896-2026 — olympic_athletes
text: 1896-2016: Griffin, R. (2018). Olympic_history.     https://github.com/rgriff23/Olympic_history 2018-2026: https://www.olympedia.org/ (scraped April 2026).

title: Olympic athlete-event participations, 1896-2026 — olympic_athletes
code: data(olympic_athletes) head(olympic_athletes) #>      id                       name sex age height weight          team noc #> 1 12068       Arthur Charles Blake   M  24     NA     NA United States USA #> 2 35094             Angelos Fetsis   M  NA     NA     NA        Greece GRE #> 3 35698 Edwin Harold "Teddy" Flack   M  22     NA     NA     Australia AUS #> 4 38123                 Carl Galle   M  23    154     45       Germany GER #> 5 41160       Dimitrios P. Golemis   M  21     NA     NA        Greece GRE #> 6 57441  Konstantinos Karakatsanis   M  NA     NA     NA        Greece GRE #>         games year season city_local_latin city_english     sport #> 1 1896 Summer 1896 Summer           Athína       Athens Athletics #> 2 1896 Summer 1896 Summer           Athína       Athens Athletics #> 3 1896 Summer 1896 Summer           Athína       Athens Athletics #> 4 1896 Summer 1896 Summer           Athína       Athens Athletics #> 5 1896 Summer 1896 Summer           Athína       Athens Athletics #> 6 1896 Summer 1896 Summer           Athína       Athens Athletics #>                          event  medal #> 1 Athletics Men's 1,500 metres Silver #> 2 Athletics Men's 1,500 metres   <NA> #> 3 Athletics Men's 1,500 metres   Gold #> 4 Athletics Men's 1,500 metres   <NA> #> 5 Athletics Men's 1,500 metres   <NA> #> 6 Athletics Men's 1,500 metres   <NA> table(olympic_athletes$year, olympic_athletes$season) #>        #>        Summer Winter #>   1896    380      0 #>   1900   1936      0 #>   1904   1301      0 #>   1906   1733      0 #>   1908   3101      0 #>   1912   4040      0 #>   1920   4292      0 #>   1924   5233    460 #>   1928   4992    582 #>   1932   2969    352 #>   1936   6506    895 #>   1948   6405   1075 #>   1952   8270   1088 #>   1956   5127   1307 #>   1960   8119   1116 #>   1964   7702   1778 #>   1968   8588   1891 #>   1972  10304   1655 #>   1976   8641   1861 #>   1980   7191   1746 #>   1984   9454   2134 #>   1988  12037   2639 #>   1992  12977   3436 #>   1994      0   3160 #>   1996  13780      0 #>   1998      0   3605 #>   2000  13821      0 #>   2002      0   4109 #>   2004  13443      0 #>   2006      0   4382 #>   2008  13602      0 #>   2010      0   4402 #>   2012  12920      0 #>   2014      0   4891 #>   2016  13688      0 #>   2018      0   5119 #>   2020  14536      0 #>   2022      0   5247 #>   2024  13659      0 #>   2026      0   5413  if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{   # Top medal-winning NOCs at Paris 2024 (one row per athlete-medal)   library(dplyr)   olympic_athletes |>     filter(year == 2024, !is.na(medal)) |>     count(noc, medal) |>     tidyr::pivot_wider(names_from = medal, values_from = n, values_fill = 0) |>     arrange(desc(Gold + Silver + Bronze)) } # }

title: olympicAthletes 0.3.0
text: Breaking: olympic_athletes columns now lowercase snake_case (id, name, sex, age, height, weight, team, noc, games, year, season, sport, event, medal), matching convention already used medal_table editions. upstream rgriff23 TitleCase headers (ID, Name, NOC, …) longer preserved. Host-city columns now come local-language English forms. olympic_athletes, City column replaced city_local_latin city_english. editions, city replaced city_local_latin city_english. city_local_latin always uses Latin alphabet — Latin-script names keep native diacritics ("Athína", "München", "Montréal", "Ciudad de México") non-Latin-script names use standard romanization ("Moskva", "Tokyo", "Beijing", "PyeongChang") rather city’s native script.

title: olympicAthletes 0.2.0
text: medal_table now covers every Olympic edition Athens 1896 Milano-Cortina 2026 (1,929 rows, 273). Two new columns: edition_id (Olympedia’s internal edition number) notes. editions now covers every Olympic edition Athens 1896 Milano-Cortina 2026, including cancelled Games (62 rows, 5).

title: olympicAthletes 0.1.0
text: Initial release. olympic_athletes — 314,749 athlete-event rows spanning Athens 1896 Milano-Cortina 2026, extending rgriff23 Olympic_history dataset (1896-2016) five additional editions scraped olympedia.org. medal_table — 273-row verified medal table five new editions (2018, 2020, 2022, 2024, 2026), one row per (Games, NOC). editions — 5-row metadata table covering opening/closing dates, participants, NOC count, medal events, disciplines new edition.
